factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly lists the Master 2 (M2) Economics application window as “February 25, 2026 – May 25, 2026,” which states both the opening and closing dates required.

factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly states the Master’s in Economics at Université Paris-Saclay is “fully taught in English” and lists “Languages of Instruction: Fully in English.”

presentation-quality.requirement: Maintains professional academic guidance tone without promotional language or casual phrasing
presentation-quality.explanation: The response uses a formal, structured report style with academic guidance language (e.g., eligibility criteria, procedures, deadlines) and avoids casual phrasing. While it includes mildly promotional terms like “prestigious” and “maximum clarity and depth,” the overall tone remains professional and non-marketing.

presentation-quality.requirement: Uses hierarchical organization with clear sections separating program admission from scholarship information
presentation-quality.explanation: The response is clearly structured with numbered headings, including a dedicated Section 1 for the Master’s in Economics admission requirements and a separate Section 2 for the France Excellence Charpak scholarship details, keeping the two topics distinct.

presentation-quality.requirement: Formats requirements, documents, and procedures as lists or bullet points for clarity
presentation-quality.explanation: The response consistently formats admission requirements, eligibility criteria, deadlines, required documents, and selection stages using bullet-point lists across multiple sections (e.g., Eligibility Criteria, Application Deadlines and Procedures, Required Documents, Selection Procedure).

presentation-quality.requirement: Restates the same eligibility criterion, deadline, or document requirement multiple times without adding new context
presentation-quality.explanation: The response repeats the same requirements and deadlines in multiple sections (e.g., eligibility criteria and deadlines for both the Master’s program and Charpak scholarship are restated again in the “Summary and Recommendations” after being detailed earlier) without adding materially new context.

presentation-quality.requirement: Uses excessive wordiness, filler phrases, or unnecessary elaboration that extends length without adding substantive information
presentation-quality.explanation: The response is highly verbose, with repeated “overview/guidance/recommendations” sections, extensive program descriptions (e.g., curriculum, assessment, ECTS, integration notes), and multiple disclaimers that go beyond the requested admissions and scholarship requirements. It includes unnecessary elaboration like “prestigious” framing and long benefit/exclusion narratives, extending length without adding core requirement details.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Université Paris-Saclay official Master in Economics program pages (such as https://www.universite-paris-saclay.fr/en/education/master/economics) for admission requirements, deadlines, or documents
citation-quality.explanation: The response includes a citation to an official Université Paris-Saclay Master’s in Economics program page ("http://www.universite-paris-saclay.fr/en/education/masters-degree/economics") in its Sources section and uses it as the basis for admission-related information.

citation-quality.requirement: References Campus France India website (campusfrance.org, https://www.inde.campusfrance.org/charpak-master-s-scholarship) for Charpak scholarship eligibility, benefits, or application process
citation-quality.explanation: The response explicitly cites the Campus France India Charpak Master’s Scholarship page and includes the URL https://inde.campusfrance.org/charpak-master-s-scholarship in the Sources section, using it as an official reference for eligibility, benefits, and application details.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Reddit, Quora, student blogs, or third-party scholarship aggregators as primary sources for eligibility criteria or deadlines
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites third-party scholarship aggregators as sources for eligibility criteria and deadlines, including LeapScholar (Source [8]) and Opportunities for Africans (Source [10]). These are used alongside official sources in the Sources section, making them part of the primary sourcing for requirements.
